content.problemStatement:
Chef is learning about **absolute** MEX sequences.

A sequence AAA of NNN integers is said to be an **absolute** MEX sequence, if the following property holds for all 1≤i≤N1\le i \le N1≤i≤N:


∣Ai−MEX(A1,A2,…,Ai)∣≤1|A_i - \texttt{MEX} (A_1, A_2, \dots, A_i)| \leq 1∣Ai​−MEX(A1​,A2​,…,Ai​)∣≤1
Chef has two integers NNN and MMM. Find the total number of **absolute** MEX sequences AAA, of length NNN, such that 0≤Ai≤M0 \leq A_i \leq M0≤Ai​≤M.

Since answer can be huge, output the answer modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.

Note:


∣X∣|X|∣X∣ denotes the absolute value of XXX. For example, ∣4∣=4|4| = 4∣4∣=4 and ∣−7∣=7|-7| = 7∣−7∣=7.
MEX(A1,A2,…,AN)\texttt{MEX} (A_1, A_2, \dots, A_N)MEX(A1​,A2​,…,AN​) denotes the MEX of the sequence [A1,A2,…,AN][A_1, A_2, \dots, A_N][A1​,A2​,…,AN​]. For example, MEX(2,2,1)=0\texttt{MEX}(2,2,1)=0MEX(2,2,1)=0, and MEX(3,1,0,1)=2\texttt{MEX}(3, 1, 0, 1)=2MEX(3,1,0,1)=2.

Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of test cases. Then, the test cases follow.
Each test case contains of a single line of input, containing two space-separated integers NNN and MMM, as mentioned in statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line, the total number of **absolute** MEX sequences satisfying the condition, modulo 109+710^9+7109+7 .
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1062 \cdot 10^62⋅106
The sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1062 \cdot 10^62⋅106
